body:
This adds behaviour for making an on-disk (and in-memory) field dataset similar to how a field timeseries can be constructed. `set!` passes through to `set!` for each timeseries corresponding to each keyword argument entered. There is also support for initializing a `FieldDataset` with entries similar a given named tuple of fields, intended for easily outputting post-processed data, for instance. 

The test is just a shameless copy of the test for `FieldTimeSeries{OnDisk}`.

In my opinion, `FieldDataset` deserves a rework. I envision it implementing `AbstractDict{String, FieldTimeSeries}`, for some set of `FieldTimeSeries` that share the same `grid`, `backend`, `times`, `path`, `reader_kw` (and possibly `time_indexing`?, though maybe there is a use case where a user wants this to differ between fields in the same file). I think this could be done without breaking changes given its only current use is reading in all timeseries in the same file, though I didn't commit to it here. It feels clunky currently. The type definition allows it to contain anything, which is why, for instance, support for writing metadata is avoided here. I'm open to working on a rework in this PR.
title: Allow initialization of empty `FieldDataset`s similar to methods for `FieldTimeSeries` and define `set!` behaviour for `FieldDataset`
Fix and improve show methods for `CATKEEquation` and `CATKEMixingLength`
